---
title: "ePortfolio: Reflection"
description: "A reflection on the skills and knowledge acquired throughout the BAA1028 Workflow & Data Management module."
author: "Daniel Vala"
date: "2026-04-05"
categories: [Reflection, Web Development, Git]
---

This module introduced me to several tools and concepts that were entirely new to me. I learned the basics of Git and GitHub for version control, which I can see being useful in any data or technology-related role in the future. More importantly, the module gave me a solid introduction to web development and web design - HTML, CSS, and Sass - which allowed me to understand how websites are built and styled from scratch.

Perhaps the most valuable takeaway was realising how easy and accessible it is to create and publish your own website. Using tools like Quarto and GitHub Pages, I was able to build a professional-looking site without any prior web development experience and at no cost. This is something I find genuinely inspiring and plan to make use of in the future.

Finally, this project gave me the opportunity to practice working with AI in a coding context. I used AI assistance throughout the development process, which significantly sped up my learning. I believe this is particularly relevant, as collaborating with AI is very likely to become a standard part of software development in the near future. Learning how to communicate with AI tools effectively, verify their output, and integrate their suggestions into my own workflow is a skill I want to continue building on.
